TML file resides on the http://ptolemy.eecs.berkeley.edu web server. See below for details. This model contains a component that is defined on the Ptolemy project website. When you open the model, you will be alerted to the fact that it requires loa

u will be alerted to the fact that it requires loading a model definition from a remote source. If you agree to proceed, then you will have a model with a remotely defined component. The component itself is at http://ptolemy.eecs.berkeley.edu/xml/mo

self is at http://ptolemy.eecs.berkeley.edu/xml/models/Waveform.xml . You can open that definition by clicking on the hyperlink, or by using the "Open URL" command in the File menu. This applet will only work if this HTML file resides on the http://p

nly work if this HTML file resides on the http://ptolemy.eecs.berkeley.edu web server. The reason is that the default applet security policy only permits connections to the machine where the applet originated. Thus, if this applet is on your local ma

iginated. Thus, if this applet is on your local machine, you will get a SecurityException when the applet tries to connect to http://ptolemy.eecs.berkeley.edu to download part of the model. This restriction applies only to running this model as an a

riction applies only to running this model as an applet, the model will run without this restriction under vergil or Web Start. The block diagram for the model was constructed using the Ptolemy II schematic editor called Vergil.
